TextENGNew: ...She couldn't remember how much she had drunk, or how many bars she had stumbled through to end up here.
When she came to, Tomoe found herself slumped on a park bench.
The area around her had gone completely dark. Not a soul in sight.
She raised her head and looked around... but through the haze of alcohol, the world lurched sickeningly before her eyes.
(If someone attacked me right now, I'd be completely defenseless...)
She smiled bitterly to herself, swallowing back a rising wave of nausea.
No matter how many times she tried to stand, her legs refused to cooperate.
"Female chief inspector, the hope of the National Police Agency, attacked and killed by a thug while drunk?@k@vS11/28/420101339. ...Haha, totally uncool."
Not that getting stabbed and collapsing in a pool of blood would be the end of it — knowing her.
She would resist with everything she had, trying to apprehend her attacker... and in doing so, probably only lower her own chances of survival.
She could easily picture the look on her sister's face — and the torrent of abuse that would follow.
changeReason: New translation includes the detail about her sister's verbal abuse (罵声を浴びせてくる), which was omitted in the original.

TextENGNew: ...You're such an idiot...
"Yeah, I'm an idiot.@k@vS11/28/420101341. ...Excuse me for being so stupid. Damn it."
Tomoe leaned back into the bench and gazed up at the night sky as she spat those words out.
...Frustrated as she was, the sky was filled with a sea of beautiful stars, shining in full view.
"...Minai-san."
The twinkling of the stars suddenly distorted into an image of Nagisa's smiling face.
The corners of her eyes began to heat up, and her chest tightened in pain.
(In the end... I couldn't protect her.@k@vS11/28/420101344. In fact, it's completely the opposite. I brought misfortune onto someone who shouldn't even have gotten involved...)
No matter how much she regretted it, it wasn't enough.
If Tomoe hadn't told her about Reina Ryuugu's whereabouts to begin with...
"...I'm a plague of destruction.@k@vS11/28/420101346. Ahah, ahahaha..."
...As the depression sank in, Tomoe's voice warped into a laugh that would likely give her a sore throat.
Up until now, there were times she felt depressed because she failed, or something didn't work out.
The sadness and regret... drove her to drink enough to lose consciousness, more than once or twice in the past.
However.
This was the first time... she lamented her own negligence and powerlessness, and hated herself so much that she wanted to hang herself by the neck.
"But if you get overly eager... it'll only cause you pain later on, you know?"
The words of her old boss at the youth division, Section Chief Kishimoto, echoed through her drunken head.
Deep down, she knew it was a bad habit to get emotional and personally attached to the ones involved.
But even so... no matter how hard she tried, Tomoe couldn't abandon that mindset.
The scars and memories from her time as a victim wouldn't allow her to do otherwise...
"...@k@vS11/28/420101348.Am I not fit to be a detective?@k@vS11/28/420101349. Is this not possible for me...?"
Then, Tomoe scooped up the can of beer she brought with her and guzzled it down her throat. ...She was long beyond the point she could even taste it.
...Tomoe was getting kicked out of Kakiuchi next month.
She hadn't even started on her father's case, and she couldn't catch the person who killed Nagisa... She was just clumsily and pitifully leaving it all behind.
At this point, she didn't even care about the hate and ridicule of Okabe and the others, saying, "Serves you right."
In fact, what she felt most sad about... was the people like Fujita, who worried about her and placed their trust in her.
"...I'm sorry, everyone."
They were good people. No matter how disappointed they were, they'd still try to encourage Tomoe.
But... just knowing that made it all the more painful.
Unfortunately, they weren't her friends. They were comrades tied by a trusting workplace relationship.
As their dependable boss, Tomoe's pride wouldn't allow them to cross that line by being overly sympathetic to her troubles.
"Who am I even trying to impress at this point...@k@vS11/28/420101352. But still, ...?"
Just then, she heard a strange sound coming from the distance.
What could it be? ...Was someone nearby?
No... that wasn't it. She felt something like this before, not that long ago.
That's right, she remembered. ...It was in Hinamizawa.
After the gas was drained from her car, when she was walking to the phone booth...
She heard the same sound back then.
When she attempted to stand up... she immediately collapsed on the spot.
She had no strength left to fight it.
Feeling like a fish on the chopping block, she gave in and thought to herself, "If you're going to kill me, do it now."
"...Whatever you want to do, fine.@k@vS11/28/420101355. I don't really care anymore."
At this point, all her hope and pride were exhausted... She was just a soulless shell who'd lost its purpose.
If you want to kill me, feel free.
If you want to sacrifice me to some curse, I'll go willingly...
The sound slowly grew louder. Pat pat, tap.
But even so, she wasn't afraid. ...Although, that may have been due to a loss of sanity in her current drunken state.
Before long.
The sound continued echoing until it was right beside Tomoe... and then, it stopped.
It certainly felt like someone was standing beside her.
However, they didn't say or do anything.
The silence continued for a while, but Tomoe eventually grew frustrated with the oppressive atmosphere and quickly turned her head to look up.
However.
There was nobody there.
In fact... when She looked at the surrounding area, it was no longer the original park where she had fallen.
"What... is this place...?"
Tomoe shook her head many times, thinking she was excessively drunk.
But despite that... an indescribable space like a fantasy world was laid out before Tomoe's eyes.
"Why on earth am I...?"
...So it was you, wasn't it?
That moment.
She could feel a voice coming not through her ears, but directly inside her head, so she quickly took a look around.
And then,.
...You're one of the most important fragments in carving out Rika's destiny.
The gap has finally been filled, but I couldn't find the missing piece at the edge no matter how hard I looked.
...But, I finally found it.
"Who... are you?!"
An unfamiliar girl dressed in shrine maiden's clothes... suddenly emerged in this empty space.
It was a face she hadn't seen before. ...And she could see things resembling horns on her head.
Who on earth was this girl...?!
...You are a mysterious being. Even though we don't know you at all... even though you seem to be a completely unrelated individual, your existence will be pivotal in turning the gears of fate.
In order to make a miracle happen, it's necessary to have a person like the joker in playing cards.
"J-joker...? And what's all this about the gears of fate?"
...The gears won't turn if the cogs are all spinning in the same direction.
They have to move in the correct way.
It won't work unless some are spinning in the opposite direction.
...The gears of fate were too strong, so the flow couldn't be changed as easily as we wanted.
So, I've been gathering small miracles here and there in order to win back Rika's happiness.
Because without that, the flow would never move in the way we envisioned...
She couldn't understand.
Tomoe couldn't understand the meaning behind what this girl was saying.
...But, I realized.
The truth is, there's still one gear that isn't moving. In order to get everything moving, the one gear that fell out of sequence needs to be reset.
It's very small, but if you put it back in place and start to move it, the other gears will move along with it.
The largest gear, which hadn't budged until now, has started to move in the opposite direction...
...And, at first glance, that movement seems to be hindering us.
But with the movement of a single gear, an inevitable miracle will put the dice of fate back in our hands.
That gear is you.
By removing the stagnation from within that fragment that we couldn't remove, you yourself will drift away with that stagnation, but you are one who can transform it into something more tangible.
Is this... a dream?
Am I witnessing some ridiculous dream??
...Tomoe Minai.
You've suffered greatly in the past, but you're still a person with a strong will to move forward.
We need you.
No, Rika needs you.
"W-wait.@k@vS11/28/420101365. Rika...? Who?@k@vS11/28/420101366. And more importantly, who on earth are you?!"
...I am... "Oyashiro-sama."
A supernatural being that you still misunderstand and continue to hate for allegedly hurting and taking those dear to you.
The blood suddenly ran to Tomoe's head and she reflexively attempted to stand up... but she lacked the strength in her legs, so she fell in place.
Still, even though she turned to the girl with rage in her eyes, the girl responded with a timid smile devoid of fear.
...Are you hateful?
Frustrated?
Yes, those dark emotions are essential to us.
Like fighting fire with fire, only strong dark emotions can resist a dark will.
...We'll be waiting for you.
In Hinamizawa...
When Tomoe came back to her senses, she found herself on a park bench.
She glanced at her watch to check the time. ...It was nearly morning.
"I fell asleep?@k@vS11/28/420101371. ...Wow, it's cold."
A chill ran all the way to her core, and her whole body suddenly began to shake. She had completely sobered up.
There was a dull pain in her head, but her thoughts were cool and collected. ...Fortunately, she didn't seem to have caught a cold, despite sleeping in a place like this.
More than that, it was a miracle that a young woman left defenseless like that was able to wake up in perfectly good health in the morning.
"A miracle...?"
It was a peculiar dream.
She couldn't remember the face of the girl who appeared in her dream... but she could vaguely recall bits and pieces of her words.
There was definitely something about turning gears around. And...
I remember. ...It was back then.
When Reina Ryuugu attacked me... that girl was talking to me as my consciousness began to fade away...
And that memory came back inside my head to form that dream.
But looking at it in hindsight, I can't deny the possibility that what I saw and heard back then was a dream too...
"Fight fire with fire...@k@vS11/28/420101374. Resist darkness with darkness?"
At first, she couldn't quite understand the meaning. But as she continued to ponder it... little by little, she was able to piece it together.
...Fight hate with hate.
It wasn't as simple as standing up and fighting with revenge in your heart. ...That simply made it a collision between two egos.
In other words, it's about approaching your opponent with the same passion, but also with understanding.
It is only because you feel your own strong dark emotions that you're in a position to understand your adversary's. And in doing so, you also know how to calm your opponent.
...Humans are not wild animals. With few exceptions, they rely on reasoning rather than instinct.
So, when a person chooses to attack another, there is a "reason" that drove them to that action.
In order to identify that, you must first know the other person and their sense of values.
Find the source of despair that nobody else could understand, and unravel the dark emotions that drove them to solitude.
That's right... because compassion and animosity are two sides of the same coin.
And, as those words repeated in Tomoe's head over and over, she realized that the hopeless despondency she had been feeling until last night was no longer there.
"I see.@k@vS11/28/420101376. ...That's how it is."
Tomoe stood up as she said that. She glanced at the empty can in her hand and then at a trash can a short distance away, then tossed it in a snap.
The can raced through the air in a slightly arcing path.
A dull sound rang out as it collided with the aluminum on the edge, and then fell right in place.
